major_tropes: The Crew That Should Not Trust Each Other But Must
The Plan That Goes Wrong in Exactly the Right Ways
The Mark Who Is More Dangerous Than Expected
plot_template: A crew of specialists is assembled for an audacious theft or con that requires every member to perform perfectly. The plan begins to fall apart at the exact wrong moments — not through incompetence, but through human variables no plan can account for. Survival requires improvisation, and improvisation reveals who everyone really is.
character_archetypes: The Mastermind Who Has More at Stake Than They've Said
The Specialist Whose Skill Hides a Liability
The Wild Card Who Is Unpredictable For a Reason
The Target Who Is Running Their Own Game
world_building_notes: The plan must be shown in enough detail that the audience understands what should happen — so they can feel the divergence when it doesn't. The setting (vault, casino, museum, ship) must be specific enough to be a puzzle. Twist reveals must be set up fairly in advance. Every crew member must want something beyond the money.
emotional_core: Trust and betrayal, competence under pressure, the one variable no plan accounts for, the thrill of the impossible
